[35m[codecompanion.nvim] [0m[36mcheckout[0m[90m | [0mstate without impacting any branches by switching back to a branch.
[35m[codecompanion.nvim] [0m[36mcheckout[0m[90m | [0m
If you want to create a new branch to retain commits you create, you may
[35m[codecompanion.nvim] [0m[36mcheckout[0m[90m | [0mdo so (now or later) by using -c with the switch command. Example:
[35m[codecompanion.nvim] [0m[36mcheckout[0m[90m | [0m

[35m[codecompanion.nvim] [0m[36mcheckout[0m[90m | [0m

[35m[codecompanion.nvim] [0m[36mcheckout[0m[90m | [0m
